adc.name: adc
adc.summary: Adds the source operand and the carry flag to the destination operand.
notes: Used for multi-precision addition after `add`.

adcx.name: adcx
adcx.summary: Adds unsigned integers using CF as the carry chain.
notes: Useful for multi-precision arithmetic with a carry chain independent of OF.

add.name: add
add.summary: Adds the source operand to the destination operand.
notes: Stores the sum in the destination operand.

addpd.name: addpd
addpd.subtitle: Add packed double-precision floating-point values
addpd.summary: Adds packed double-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

addps.name: addps
addps.subtitle: Add packed single-precision floating-point values
addps.summary: Adds packed single-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

addsd.name: addsd
addsd.subtitle: Add scalar double-precision floating-point values
addsd.summary: Adds scalar double-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

addss.name: addss
addss.subtitle: Add scalar single-precision floating-point values
addss.summary: Adds scalar single-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

adox.name: adox
adox.summary: Adds unsigned integers using OF as the carry chain.
notes: Often paired with `adcx` for two independent carry chains.

aesdec.name: aesdec
notes: Used by optimized cryptographic implementations.

aesdeclast.name: aesdeclast
aesdeclast.summary: Performs the final AES decryption round.
notes: Used by optimized cryptographic implementations.

aesenc.name: aesenc
notes: Used by optimized cryptographic implementations.

aesenclast.name: aesenclast
aesenclast.summary: Performs the final AES encryption round.
notes: Used by optimized cryptographic implementations.

aesimc.name: aesimc
aesimc.summary: Applies the inverse mix-columns transform to an AES round key.
notes: Used by optimized cryptographic implementations.

aeskeygenassist.name: aeskeygenassist
aeskeygenassist.summary: Assists AES round-key generation using an immediate round constant.
notes: Used by optimized cryptographic implementations.

and.name: and
and.summary: Computes bitwise AND and stores the result in the destination operand.
notes: Often used for masking bits.

andn.name: andn
andn.summary: Computes bitwise AND NOT: destination = (~source1) & source2.
notes: BMI1 instruction; unlike `and`, the destination is separate from both inputs.

andnpd.name: andnpd
andnpd.summary: Computes bitwise AND NOT on packed double-precision floating-point vector data.
notes: The operation treats the vector bits directly; it does not perform numeric floating-point arithmetic.

andnps.name: andnps
andnps.summary: Computes bitwise AND NOT on packed single-precision floating-point vector data.
notes: The operation treats the vector bits directly; it does not perform numeric floating-point arithmetic.

andpd.name: andpd
andpd.summary: Computes bitwise AND on packed double-precision floating-point vector data.
notes: The operation treats the vector bits directly; it does not perform numeric floating-point arithmetic.

andps.name: andps
andps.summary: Computes bitwise AND on packed single-precision floating-point vector data.
notes: The operation treats the vector bits directly; it does not perform numeric floating-point arithmetic.

bextr.name: bextr
bextr.summary: Extracts a contiguous bit field from a source operand.
notes: The control operand encodes start bit and length.

blsi.name: blsi
blsi.summary: Extracts the lowest set bit from the source operand.
notes: Result is zero if the source is zero.

blsmsk.name: blsmsk
blsmsk.summary: Creates a mask from bit 0 through the lowest set bit of the source.
notes: Useful for bitset iteration.

blsr.name: blsr
blsr.summary: Clears the lowest set bit of the source operand.
notes: Useful for iterating over set bits.

bndcl.name: bndcl
bndcl.summary: Checks a pointer against the lower bound in a bounds register.
notes: Intel MPX/bounds instruction; uncommon in modern user code.

bndcn.name: bndcn
bndcn.summary: Checks a pointer against the complement upper bound in a bounds register.
notes: Intel MPX/bounds instruction; uncommon in modern user code.

bndcu.name: bndcu
bndcu.summary: Checks a pointer against the upper bound in a bounds register.
notes: Intel MPX/bounds instruction; uncommon in modern user code.

bndmk.name: bndmk
bndmk.summary: Creates bounds in a bounds register from a memory address expression.
notes: Intel MPX/bounds instruction; uncommon in modern user code.

bndmov.name: bndmov
bndmov.summary: Moves bounds-register data between bounds registers or memory.
notes: Intel MPX/bounds instruction; uncommon in modern user code.

bsf.name: bsf
bsf.summary: Scans from low to high for the first set bit.
notes: ZF is set when the source is zero; otherwise the bit index is written to the destination.

bsr.name: bsr
bsr.summary: Scans from high to low for the first set bit.
notes: ZF is set when the source is zero; otherwise the bit index is written to the destination.

bt.name: bt
bt.summary: Copies a selected bit into the carry flag.
notes: The source operand is not modified.

btc.name: btc
btc.summary: Copies a selected bit into CF and complements that bit in the destination.
notes: Can be used for atomic bit toggles with `lock` when the destination is memory.

btr.name: btr
btr.summary: Copies a selected bit into CF and resets that bit in the destination.
notes: Can be used for atomic bit clears with `lock` when the destination is memory.

bts.name: bts
bts.summary: Copies a selected bit into CF and sets that bit in the destination.
notes: Can be used for atomic bit sets with `lock` when the destination is memory.

bzhi.name: bzhi
bzhi.summary: Zeros high bits above a selected bit index.
notes: BMI2 instruction.

call.name: call
call.summary: Calls a procedure and saves the return address on the stack.
notes: Use `ret` in the callee to return.

cbw.name: cbw
notes: Used before signed byte division with `idiv r/m8`.

cdq.name: cdq
cdq.summary: Sign-extends EAX into EDX:EAX for signed 32-bit division.
notes: Use before `idiv r/m32` when the dividend is in `eax`.

cdqe.name: cdqe
notes: Common after producing a signed 32-bit result that must be used as a 64-bit value.

clc.name: clc
notes: Often used before multi-precision arithmetic.

cld.name: cld
cld.summary: Clears the direction flag so string instructions advance addresses upward.
notes: Common before `rep movsb` or other forward string operations.

clflush.name: clflush
clflush.summary: Flushes the cache line containing the memory operand.
notes: Used for cache management and some memory-mapped I/O workflows.

clflushopt.name: clflushopt
clflushopt.summary: Flushes a cache line with weaker ordering than `clflush`.
notes: Use fences when ordering with other memory operations matters.

cli.name: cli
cli.summary: Clears the interrupt flag, disabling maskable interrupts.
notes: Privileged in normal operating-system environments.

clwb.name: clwb
clwb.summary: Writes back a cache line to memory without necessarily invalidating it.
notes: Used with persistent memory or explicit cache-management code.

cmc.name: cmc
notes: Flips CF from 0 to 1 or 1 to 0.

cmova.name: cmova
cmova.summary: Copies the source to the destination if unsigned above is true.
notes: Only the destination register is changed when the condition is true.
The source may be a register or memory operand.

cmovae.name: cmovae
cmovae.summary: Copies the source to the destination if unsigned above or equal is true.
notes: Only the destination register is changed when the condition is true.
The source may be a register or memory operand.

cmovb.name: cmovb
cmovb.summary: Copies the source to the destination if unsigned below is true.
notes: Only the destination register is changed when the condition is true.
The source may be a register or memory operand.

cmovbe.name: cmovbe
cmovbe.summary: Copies the source to the destination if unsigned below or equal is true.
notes: Only the destination register is changed when the condition is true.
The source may be a register or memory operand.

cmovg.name: cmovg
cmovg.summary: Copies the source to the destination if signed greater is true.
notes: Only the destination register is changed when the condition is true.
The source may be a register or memory operand.

cmovge.name: cmovge
cmovge.summary: Copies the source to the destination if signed greater or equal is true.
notes: Only the destination register is changed when the condition is true.
The source may be a register or memory operand.

cmovl.name: cmovl
cmovl.summary: Copies the source to the destination if signed less is true.
notes: Only the destination register is changed when the condition is true.
The source may be a register or memory operand.

cmovle.name: cmovle
cmovle.summary: Copies the source to the destination if signed less or equal is true.
notes: Only the destination register is changed when the condition is true.
The source may be a register or memory operand.

cmovno.name: cmovno
cmovno.summary: Copies the source to the destination if not overflow is true.
notes: Only the destination register is changed when the condition is true.
The source may be a register or memory operand.

cmovnp.name: cmovnp
cmovnp.summary: Copies the source to the destination if parity odd is true.
notes: Only the destination register is changed when the condition is true.
The source may be a register or memory operand.

cmovns.name: cmovns
cmovns.summary: Copies the source to the destination if not negative/not sign is true.
notes: Only the destination register is changed when the condition is true.
The source may be a register or memory operand.

cmovnz.name: cmovnz
cmovnz.summary: Copies the source to the destination if not zero/not equal is true.
notes: Only the destination register is changed when the condition is true.
The source may be a register or memory operand.

cmovo.name: cmovo
cmovo.summary: Copies the source to the destination if overflow is true.
notes: Only the destination register is changed when the condition is true.
The source may be a register or memory operand.

cmovp.name: cmovp
cmovp.summary: Copies the source to the destination if parity even is true.
notes: Only the destination register is changed when the condition is true.
The source may be a register or memory operand.

cmovs.name: cmovs
cmovs.summary: Copies the source to the destination if negative/sign is true.
notes: Only the destination register is changed when the condition is true.
The source may be a register or memory operand.

cmovz.name: cmovz
cmovz.summary: Copies the source to the destination if zero/equal is true.
notes: Only the destination register is changed when the condition is true.
The source may be a register or memory operand.

cmp.name: cmp
cmp.summary: Subtracts the source from the destination only to update CPU flags. The result is not stored.
notes: `je` checks ZF = 1.
`jg` checks signed greater-than.
`ja` checks unsigned above.

cmppd.name: cmppd
cmppd.summary: Compares packed double-precision floating-point values using an immediate predicate and writes comparison masks to the destination.
notes: The immediate selects the comparison predicate such as equal or less-than.

cmpps.name: cmpps
cmpps.summary: Compares packed single-precision floating-point values using an immediate predicate and writes comparison masks to the destination.
notes: The immediate selects the comparison predicate such as equal or less-than.

cmps.name: cmps
cmps.summary: Compares elements at [RSI] and [RDI], updates flags, and updates both pointers.
notes: Element size is selected by the operand-size attribute or by using an explicit suffix.

cmpsb.name: cmpsb
cmpsb.summary: Compares one byte at [RSI] with one byte at [RDI], updates flags, and updates both pointers.
notes: Often used with `repe` or `repne` for memory comparisons.

cmpsd.name: cmpsd
cmpsd.summary: Either compares string doublewords (`cmpsd` with no operands) or compares scalar double-precision floating-point values in SSE forms.
notes: NASM/x86 overloads this mnemonic; operands determine the instruction form.

cmpsq.name: cmpsq
cmpsq.summary: Compares one quadword at [RSI] with one quadword at [RDI], updates flags, and updates both pointers.
notes: Often used with `repe` or `repne` for memory comparisons.

cmpss.name: cmpss
cmpss.summary: Compares scalar single-precision floating-point values using an immediate predicate and writes comparison masks to the destination.
notes: The immediate selects the comparison predicate such as equal or less-than.

cmpsw.name: cmpsw
cmpsw.summary: Compares one word at [RSI] with one word at [RDI], updates flags, and updates both pointers.
notes: Often used with `repe` or `repne` for memory comparisons.

cmpxchg.name: cmpxchg
cmpxchg.summary: Compares the accumulator with the destination and stores the source if they are equal.
notes: Sets ZF when the exchange succeeds.
With a memory destination, use `lock` for atomic compare-and-swap.

cmpxchg16b.name: cmpxchg16b
cmpxchg16b.summary: Atomically compares RDX:RAX with a 128-bit memory value and exchanges from RCX:RBX on success.
notes: Sets ZF when the exchange succeeds.
Requires a 16-byte memory operand.

cmpxchg8b.name: cmpxchg8b
cmpxchg8b.summary: Atomically compares EDX:EAX with a 64-bit memory value and exchanges from ECX:EBX on success.
notes: Sets ZF when the exchange succeeds.

comisd.name: comisd
comisd.summary: Compares scalar double-precision floating-point values and updates integer status flags.
notes: Ordered compare may raise invalid-operation for NaN inputs.

comiss.name: comiss
comiss.summary: Compares scalar single-precision floating-point values and updates integer status flags.
notes: Ordered compare may raise invalid-operation for NaN inputs.

cpuid.name: cpuid
cpuid.summary: Returns processor feature and identification information selected by EAX and sometimes ECX.
notes: Results are returned in EAX, EBX, ECX, and EDX.

cqo.name: cqo
cqo.summary: Sign-extends RAX into RDX:RAX for signed 64-bit division.
notes: Use before `idiv r/m64` when the dividend is in `rax`.

cwd.name: cwd
notes: Used before signed word division with `idiv r/m16`.

cwde.name: cwde
notes: Converts a signed 16-bit value in AX to 32 bits in EAX.

dec.name: dec
notes: Does not update the carry flag.

div.name: div
notes: Dividend uses `rdx:rax` for 64-bit division.
Use `idiv` for signed division.

divpd.name: divpd
divpd.subtitle: Divide packed double-precision floating-point values
divpd.summary: Divides packed double-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

divps.name: divps
divps.subtitle: Divide packed single-precision floating-point values
divps.summary: Divides packed single-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

divsd.name: divsd
divsd.subtitle: Divide scalar double-precision floating-point values
divsd.summary: Divides scalar double-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

divss.name: divss
divss.subtitle: Divide scalar single-precision floating-point values
divss.summary: Divides scalar single-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

emms.name: emms
emms.summary: Marks MMX state as empty so x87 floating-point instructions can be used normally again.
notes: Use after MMX code before x87 code.

enter.name: enter
enter.summary: Creates a stack frame by saving the old base pointer and reserving stack space.
notes: Equivalent in simple cases to `push rbp`, `mov rbp, rsp`, then `sub rsp, size`.
Often avoided in optimized x86-64 code in favor of explicit prologue instructions.

f2xm1.name: f2xm1
f2xm1.summary: Computes 2^x - 1 for ST0, for inputs in the supported range.
notes: Legacy x87 floating-point instruction.

fabs.name: fabs
fabs.summary: Replaces ST0 with its absolute value.
notes: Legacy x87 floating-point instruction.

fadd.name: fadd
notes: Works with the x87 register stack or memory operands depending on form.

faddp.name: faddp
faddp.summary: Adds x87 stack values and pops the stack.
notes: Stores the result in the destination and pops ST0.

fbld.name: fbld
fbld.summary: Loads a packed BCD integer into ST0 as a floating-point value.
notes: Legacy decimal conversion helper.

fbstp.name: fbstp
fbstp.summary: Stores ST0 as packed BCD and pops the x87 stack.
notes: Legacy decimal conversion helper.

fchs.name: fchs
notes: Legacy x87 floating-point instruction.

fclex.name: fclex
fclex.summary: Clears x87 exception flags after waiting for pending exceptions.
notes: Use `fnclex` for the no-wait form.

fcmovb.name: fcmovb
fcmovb.summary: Conditionally copies an x87 register to ST0 based on integer status flags.
notes: Condition codes use the normal EFLAGS state, often from a preceding compare.

fcmovbe.name: fcmovbe
fcmovbe.summary: Conditionally copies an x87 register to ST0 based on integer status flags.
notes: Condition codes use the normal EFLAGS state, often from a preceding compare.

fcmove.name: fcmove
fcmove.summary: Conditionally copies an x87 register to ST0 based on integer status flags.
notes: Condition codes use the normal EFLAGS state, often from a preceding compare.

fcmovnb.name: fcmovnb
fcmovnb.summary: Conditionally copies an x87 register to ST0 based on integer status flags.
notes: Condition codes use the normal EFLAGS state, often from a preceding compare.

fcmovnbe.name: fcmovnbe
fcmovnbe.summary: Conditionally copies an x87 register to ST0 based on integer status flags.
notes: Condition codes use the normal EFLAGS state, often from a preceding compare.

fcmovne.name: fcmovne
fcmovne.summary: Conditionally copies an x87 register to ST0 based on integer status flags.
notes: Condition codes use the normal EFLAGS state, often from a preceding compare.

fcmovnu.name: fcmovnu
fcmovnu.summary: Conditionally copies an x87 register to ST0 based on integer status flags.
notes: Condition codes use the normal EFLAGS state, often from a preceding compare.

fcmovu.name: fcmovu
fcmovu.summary: Conditionally copies an x87 register to ST0 based on integer status flags.
notes: Condition codes use the normal EFLAGS state, often from a preceding compare.

fcomi.name: fcomi
fcomi.summary: Compares ST0 with another x87 register and updates integer flags.
notes: Useful before normal conditional jumps.

fcomip.name: fcomip
fcomip.summary: Compares ST0 with another x87 register, updates integer flags, and pops ST0.
notes: Useful before normal conditional jumps.

fcomp.name: fcomp
fcomp.summary: Compares x87 values and pops one stack entry.
notes: Updates x87 status flags, not normal integer EFLAGS directly.

fcompp.name: fcompp
fcompp.summary: Compares x87 values and pops two stack entries.
notes: Updates x87 status flags, not normal integer EFLAGS directly.

fcos.name: fcos
notes: Legacy x87 floating-point instruction.

fdiv.name: fdiv
notes: Works with the x87 register stack or memory operands depending on form.

fdivp.name: fdivp
fdivp.summary: Divides x87 stack values and pops the stack.
notes: Stores the result in the destination and pops ST0.

fdivr.name: fdivr
notes: Works with the x87 register stack or memory operands depending on form.

fdivrp.name: fdivrp
fdivrp.summary: Reverse-divides x87 stack values and pops the stack.
notes: Stores the result in the destination and pops ST0.

ffree.name: ffree
ffree.summary: Marks an x87 stack register as empty.
notes: Does not pop; it changes the tag for the selected x87 register.

fiadd.name: fiadd
fiadd.summary: Adds an integer memory operand with ST0.
notes: The integer memory operand is converted to x87 floating-point format for the operation.

ficom.name: ficom
ficom.summary: Compares ST0 with an integer memory operand.
notes: Updates x87 status flags, not normal integer EFLAGS directly.

ficomp.name: ficomp
ficomp.summary: Compares ST0 with an integer memory operand and pops the x87 stack.
notes: Updates x87 status flags, not normal integer EFLAGS directly.

fidiv.name: fidiv
fidiv.summary: Divides an integer memory operand with ST0.
notes: The integer memory operand is converted to x87 floating-point format for the operation.

fidivr.name: fidivr
fidivr.summary: Reverse-divides an integer memory operand with ST0.
notes: The integer memory operand is converted to x87 floating-point format for the operation.

fild.name: fild
fild.summary: Loads an integer memory operand onto the x87 stack as a floating-point value.
notes: The source must be an integer memory operand.

fimul.name: fimul
fimul.summary: Multiplies an integer memory operand with ST0.
notes: The integer memory operand is converted to x87 floating-point format for the operation.

finit.name: finit
notes: Also see `fninit` for the no-wait form.

fist.name: fist
fist.summary: Stores ST0 as an integer without popping the x87 stack.
notes: Rounding follows the current x87 control word.

fistp.name: fistp
fistp.summary: Stores ST0 as an integer and pops the x87 stack.
notes: Rounding follows the current x87 control word.

fisttp.name: fisttp
fisttp.summary: Stores ST0 as an integer with truncation and pops the x87 stack.
notes: Ignores the x87 rounding-control mode and truncates toward zero.

fisub.name: fisub
fisub.summary: Subtracts an integer memory operand with ST0.
notes: The integer memory operand is converted to x87 floating-point format for the operation.

fisubr.name: fisubr
fisubr.summary: Reverse-subtracts an integer memory operand with ST0.
notes: The integer memory operand is converted to x87 floating-point format for the operation.

fld.name: fld
fld.summary: Loads a floating-point value onto the x87 register stack.
notes: The loaded value becomes ST0.

fld1.name: fld1
fld1.summary: Pushes the x87 constant 1.0 onto the floating-point stack.
notes: One of the x87 built-in constant load instructions.

fldcw.name: fldcw
fldcw.summary: Loads the x87 control word from memory.
notes: Controls rounding and exception masks.

fldenv.name: fldenv
fldenv.summary: Loads the x87 environment from memory.
notes: Restores x87 control, status, tag, and pointer state.

fldl2e.name: fldl2e
fldl2e.summary: Pushes the x87 constant log2(e) onto the floating-point stack.
notes: One of the x87 built-in constant load instructions.

fldl2t.name: fldl2t
fldl2t.summary: Pushes the x87 constant log2(10) onto the floating-point stack.
notes: One of the x87 built-in constant load instructions.

fldlg2.name: fldlg2
fldlg2.summary: Pushes the x87 constant log10(2) onto the floating-point stack.
notes: One of the x87 built-in constant load instructions.

fldln2.name: fldln2
fldln2.summary: Pushes the x87 constant ln(2) onto the floating-point stack.
notes: One of the x87 built-in constant load instructions.

fldpi.name: fldpi
fldpi.summary: Pushes the x87 constant pi onto the floating-point stack.
notes: One of the x87 built-in constant load instructions.

fldz.name: fldz
fldz.summary: Pushes the x87 constant 0.0 onto the floating-point stack.
notes: One of the x87 built-in constant load instructions.

fmul.name: fmul
notes: Works with the x87 register stack or memory operands depending on form.

fmulp.name: fmulp
fmulp.summary: Multiplies x87 stack values and pops the stack.
notes: Stores the result in the destination and pops ST0.

fnclex.name: fnclex
fnclex.summary: Clears x87 exception flags without first waiting for pending exceptions.
notes: No-wait form of `fclex`.

fninit.name: fninit
fninit.summary: Initializes the x87 floating-point unit without first waiting for pending exceptions.
notes: No-wait form of `finit`.

fnop.name: fnop
notes: Legacy x87 floating-point instruction.

fnsave.name: fnsave
fnsave.summary: Stores x87 state to memory without first waiting, then reinitializes the x87 unit.
notes: Legacy x87 state-save instruction.

fnstcw.name: fnstcw
fnstcw.summary: Stores the x87 control word to memory without first waiting for pending exceptions.
notes: No-wait form of `fstcw`.

fnstenv.name: fnstenv
fnstenv.summary: Stores the x87 environment to memory without first waiting for pending exceptions.
notes: No-wait form of `fstenv`.

fnstsw.name: fnstsw
fnstsw.summary: Stores the x87 status word without first waiting for pending exceptions.
notes: Often followed by flag-transfer logic in legacy x87 code.

fpatan.name: fpatan
fpatan.summary: Computes arctangent using ST1 and ST0, then pops the stack.
notes: Legacy x87 floating-point instruction.

fprem.name: fprem
notes: Legacy x87 floating-point instruction.

fprem1.name: fprem1
fprem1.summary: Computes an IEEE-style partial floating-point remainder.
notes: Legacy x87 floating-point instruction.

fptan.name: fptan
fptan.summary: Computes tangent of ST0 and pushes 1.0 as part of the x87 result convention.
notes: Legacy x87 floating-point instruction.

frndint.name: frndint
frndint.summary: Rounds ST0 to an integer value according to the x87 rounding mode.
notes: Legacy x87 floating-point instruction.

frstor.name: frstor
notes: Restores state saved by `fsave`/`fnsave`.

fsave.name: fsave
fsave.summary: Stores x87 state to memory after waiting, then reinitializes the x87 unit.
notes: Use `fnsave` for the no-wait form.

fscale.name: fscale
fscale.summary: Scales ST0 by a power of two derived from ST1.
notes: Legacy x87 floating-point instruction.

fsin.name: fsin
notes: Legacy x87 floating-point instruction.

fsincos.name: fsincos
fsincos.summary: Computes sine and cosine, leaving both values on the x87 stack.
notes: Legacy x87 floating-point instruction.

fsqrt.name: fsqrt
fsqrt.summary: Computes the square root of ST0.
notes: Legacy x87 floating-point instruction.

fst.name: fst
fst.summary: Stores ST0 to memory or another x87 register without popping the stack.
notes: Use `fstp` if the value should be popped after storing.

fstcw.name: fstcw
fstcw.summary: Stores the x87 control word to memory after waiting for pending exceptions.
notes: Use `fnstcw` for the no-wait form.

fstenv.name: fstenv
fstenv.summary: Stores the x87 environment to memory after waiting for pending exceptions.
notes: Use `fnstenv` for the no-wait form.

fstp.name: fstp
fstp.summary: Stores ST0 to memory or another x87 register and pops the x87 stack.
notes: Use `fst` if the value should remain on the x87 stack.

fstsw.name: fstsw
fstsw.summary: Stores the x87 status word after waiting for pending exceptions.
notes: Use `fnstsw` for the no-wait form.

fsub.name: fsub
notes: Works with the x87 register stack or memory operands depending on form.

fsubp.name: fsubp
fsubp.summary: Subtracts x87 stack values and pops the stack.
notes: Stores the result in the destination and pops ST0.

fsubr.name: fsubr
notes: Works with the x87 register stack or memory operands depending on form.

fsubrp.name: fsubrp
fsubrp.summary: Reverse-subtracts x87 stack values and pops the stack.
notes: Stores the result in the destination and pops ST0.

ftst.name: ftst
ftst.summary: Compares ST0 with 0.0 and updates x87 status flags.
notes: Legacy x87 floating-point instruction.

fucomi.name: fucomi
fucomi.summary: Unordered-compares ST0 with another x87 register and updates integer flags.
notes: Handles unordered floating-point values differently from ordered compare.

fucomip.name: fucomip
fucomip.summary: Unordered-compares ST0 with another x87 register, updates integer flags, and pops ST0.
notes: Handles unordered floating-point values differently from ordered compare.

fucomp.name: fucomp
fucomp.summary: Unordered-compares x87 values and pops one stack entry.
notes: Updates x87 status flags, not normal integer EFLAGS directly.

fucompp.name: fucompp
fucompp.summary: Unordered-compares x87 values and pops two stack entries.
notes: Updates x87 status flags, not normal integer EFLAGS directly.

fxam.name: fxam
fxam.summary: Examines the class of ST0 and updates x87 status flags.
notes: Legacy x87 floating-point instruction.

fxch.name: fxch
fxch.summary: Exchanges ST0 with another x87 stack register.
notes: Legacy x87 floating-point instruction.

fxrstor.name: fxrstor
fxrstor.summary: Restores x87, MMX, SSE, and related processor state from memory.
notes: Pairs with `fxsave`.

fxsave.name: fxsave
fxsave.summary: Saves x87, MMX, SSE, and related processor state to memory.
notes: Memory operand must use the required alignment for the CPU mode.

fxtract.name: fxtract
fxtract.summary: Extracts exponent and significand from ST0.
notes: Legacy x87 floating-point instruction.

fyl2x.name: fyl2x
fyl2x.summary: Computes ST1 * log2(ST0), then pops the stack.
notes: Legacy x87 floating-point instruction.

fyl2xp1.name: fyl2xp1
fyl2xp1.summary: Computes ST1 * log2(ST0 + 1), then pops the stack.
notes: Legacy x87 floating-point instruction.

hlt.name: hlt
hlt.summary: Halts the processor until the next external interrupt or reset event.
notes: Privileged in normal operating-system environments.

idiv.name: idiv
notes: For 64-bit operands, divides `rdx:rax` by the source.
Use `cqo` before 64-bit signed division to sign-extend `rax` into `rdx`.

imul.name: imul
notes: Use `mul` for unsigned multiplication.

in.name: in
in.summary: Reads a value from an I/O port into AL, AX, or EAX.
notes: Privileged or restricted in most protected operating systems.

inc.name: inc
notes: Does not update the carry flag.

int.name: int
int.summary: Raises a software interrupt with an immediate interrupt vector.
notes: On modern x86-64 Linux, normal syscalls use `syscall`; `int 0x80` is mostly a compatibility path.

invlpg.name: invlpg
invlpg.summary: Invalidates the TLB entry for a memory page.
notes: Privileged instruction used by kernels.

iret.name: iret
iret.summary: Returns from an interrupt or exception handler.
notes: Restores instruction pointer, code segment, and flags from the stack.

iretq.name: iretq
iretq.summary: Returns from a 64-bit interrupt or exception handler.
notes: Kernel/low-level instruction that restores RIP, CS, and RFLAGS.

ja.name: ja
ja.summary: Jumps if unsigned above: CF = 0 and ZF = 0.
notes: Use after `cmp` for unsigned greater-than checks.

jae.name: jae
jae.summary: Jumps if unsigned above or equal: CF = 0.
notes: Also called not below/not carry.

jb.name: jb
notes: Also called carry or not above-or-equal.

jbe.name: jbe
jbe.summary: Jumps if unsigned below or equal: CF = 1 or ZF = 1.
notes: Also called not above.

jcxz.name: jcxz
notes: Uses the 16-bit count register selected by address size.

jecxz.name: jecxz
notes: Often used before a loop when the count might be zero.

jg.name: jg
jg.summary: Jumps if signed greater-than: ZF = 0 and SF = OF.
notes: Use after `cmp` for signed greater-than checks.

jge.name: jge
jge.summary: Jumps if signed greater-than or equal: SF = OF.
notes: Use after `cmp` for signed greater-or-equal checks.

jl.name: jl
jl.summary: Jumps if signed less-than: SF != OF.
notes: Use after `cmp` for signed less-than checks.

jle.name: jle
jle.summary: Jumps if signed less-than or equal: ZF = 1 or SF != OF.
notes: Use after `cmp` for signed less-or-equal checks.

jmp.name: jmp
jmp.summary: Transfers control unconditionally to a target.
notes: Targets may be labels, registers, or memory operands depending on form.

jno.name: jno
jno.summary: Jumps if the overflow flag is clear.
notes: Useful after signed arithmetic when overflow matters.

jnp.name: jnp
jnp.summary: Jumps if the parity flag is clear.
notes: Also written as parity odd (`po`).

jns.name: jns
jns.summary: Jumps if the sign flag is clear.
notes: Usually means the last result had its sign bit clear.

jnz.name: jnz
jnz.summary: Jumps if the zero flag is clear.
notes: `jne` and `jnz` test the same condition.

jo.name: jo
jo.summary: Jumps if the overflow flag is set.
notes: Useful after signed arithmetic when overflow matters.

jp.name: jp
jp.summary: Jumps if the parity flag is set.
notes: Also written as parity even (`pe`).

jrcxz.name: jrcxz
notes: 64-bit count-register form.

js.name: js
js.summary: Jumps if the sign flag is set.
notes: Usually means the last result had its sign bit set.

jz.name: jz
jz.summary: Jumps if the zero flag is set.
notes: `je` and `jz` test the same condition.

kaddb.name: kaddb
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kaddd.name: kaddd
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kaddq.name: kaddq
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kaddw.name: kaddw
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kandb.name: kandb
kandb.summary: Computes bitwise AND on 8-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kandd.name: kandd
kandd.summary: Computes bitwise AND on 32-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kandnb.name: kandnb
kandnb.summary: Computes bitwise AND NOT on 8-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kandnd.name: kandnd
kandnd.summary: Computes bitwise AND NOT on 32-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kandnq.name: kandnq
kandnq.summary: Computes bitwise AND NOT on 64-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kandnw.name: kandnw
kandnw.summary: Computes bitwise AND NOT on 16-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kandq.name: kandq
kandq.summary: Computes bitwise AND on 64-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kandw.name: kandw
kandw.summary: Computes bitwise AND on 16-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kmovb.name: kmovb
kmovb.summary: Moves a 8-bit value between opmask registers, general-purpose registers, or memory.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kmovd.name: kmovd
kmovd.summary: Moves a 32-bit value between opmask registers, general-purpose registers, or memory.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kmovq.name: kmovq
kmovq.summary: Moves a 64-bit value between opmask registers, general-purpose registers, or memory.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kmovw.name: kmovw
kmovw.summary: Moves a 16-bit value between opmask registers, general-purpose registers, or memory.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

knorb.name: knorb
knorb.summary: Computes bitwise NOR on 8-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

knord.name: knord
knord.summary: Computes bitwise NOR on 32-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

knorq.name: knorq
knorq.summary: Computes bitwise NOR on 64-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

knorw.name: knorw
knorw.summary: Computes bitwise NOR on 16-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

knotb.name: knotb
knotb.summary: Computes bitwise NOT on a 8-bit opmask value.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

knotd.name: knotd
knotd.summary: Computes bitwise NOT on a 32-bit opmask value.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

knotq.name: knotq
knotq.summary: Computes bitwise NOT on a 64-bit opmask value.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

knotw.name: knotw
knotw.summary: Computes bitwise NOT on a 16-bit opmask value.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

korb.name: korb
korb.summary: Computes bitwise OR on 8-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kord.name: kord
kord.summary: Computes bitwise OR on 32-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

korq.name: korq
korq.summary: Computes bitwise OR on 64-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kortestb.name: kortestb
kortestb.summary: Tests 8-bit opmask bits and updates flags.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kortestd.name: kortestd
kortestd.summary: Tests 32-bit opmask bits and updates flags.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kortestq.name: kortestq
kortestq.summary: Tests 64-bit opmask bits and updates flags.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kortestw.name: kortestw
kortestw.summary: Tests 16-bit opmask bits and updates flags.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

korw.name: korw
korw.summary: Computes bitwise OR on 16-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kshiftlb.name: kshiftlb
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kshiftld.name: kshiftld
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kshiftlq.name: kshiftlq
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kshiftlw.name: kshiftlw
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kshiftrb.name: kshiftrb
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kshiftrd.name: kshiftrd
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kshiftrq.name: kshiftrq
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kshiftrw.name: kshiftrw
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

ktestb.name: ktestb
ktestb.summary: Tests 8-bit opmask bits and updates flags.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

ktestd.name: ktestd
ktestd.summary: Tests 32-bit opmask bits and updates flags.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

ktestq.name: ktestq
ktestq.summary: Tests 64-bit opmask bits and updates flags.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

ktestw.name: ktestw
ktestw.summary: Tests 16-bit opmask bits and updates flags.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kunpckbw.name: kunpckbw
kunpckbw.summary: Unpacks and combines opmask register bits into a wider opmask value.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kunpckdq.name: kunpckdq
kunpckdq.summary: Unpacks and combines opmask register bits into a wider opmask value.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kunpckwd.name: kunpckwd
kunpckwd.summary: Unpacks and combines opmask register bits into a wider opmask value.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kxnorb.name: kxnorb
kxnorb.summary: Computes bitwise XNOR on 8-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kxnord.name: kxnord
kxnord.summary: Computes bitwise XNOR on 32-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kxnorq.name: kxnorq
kxnorq.summary: Computes bitwise XNOR on 64-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kxnorw.name: kxnorw
kxnorw.summary: Computes bitwise XNOR on 16-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kxorb.name: kxorb
kxorb.summary: Computes bitwise XOR on 8-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kxord.name: kxord
kxord.summary: Computes bitwise XOR on 32-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kxorq.name: kxorq
kxorq.summary: Computes bitwise XOR on 64-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

kxorw.name: kxorw
kxorw.summary: Computes bitwise XOR on 16-bit opmask values.
notes: Opmask registers `k0` through `k7` control AVX-512 lane predication.

lahf.name: lahf
lahf.summary: Loads selected status flags into AH.
notes: Copies SF, ZF, AF, PF, and CF into AH.

lea.name: lea
lea.summary: Loads an effective address into a register without reading memory.
notes: Useful for address arithmetic and RIP-relative symbol addresses.

leave.name: leave
leave.subtitle: Destroy stack frame
leave.summary: Restores the stack frame created by a typical function prologue.
notes: Equivalent to `mov rsp, rbp` followed by `pop rbp`.
Restores `rsp` from `rbp`.
Pops the previous base pointer into `rbp`.
Usually followed by `ret`.

lfence.name: lfence
lfence.summary: Serializes load operations before later loads.
notes: Also commonly used as an ordering barrier before `rdtsc` on modern x86.

lgdt.name: lgdt
lgdt.summary: Loads the global descriptor table register from memory.
notes: Privileged/low-level system instruction.

lidt.name: lidt
lidt.summary: Loads the interrupt descriptor table register from memory.
notes: Privileged/low-level system instruction.

lock.name: lock
lock.summary: Requests atomic read-modify-write behavior for supported memory instructions.
notes: Only valid with selected instructions that write memory.

lods.name: lods
lods.summary: Loads one element from [RSI] into the accumulator and updates RSI.
notes: Element size is selected by the operand-size attribute or by using an explicit suffix.

lodsb.name: lodsb
lodsb.summary: Loads one byte from [RSI] into the accumulator and updates RSI.
notes: Direction depends on DF; `cld` makes RSI advance.

lodsd.name: lodsd
lodsd.summary: Loads one doubleword from [RSI] into the accumulator and updates RSI.
notes: Direction depends on DF; `cld` makes RSI advance.

lodsq.name: lodsq
lodsq.summary: Loads one quadword from [RSI] into the accumulator and updates RSI.
notes: Direction depends on DF; `cld` makes RSI advance.

lodsw.name: lodsw
lodsw.summary: Loads one word from [RSI] into the accumulator and updates RSI.
notes: Direction depends on DF; `cld` makes RSI advance.

loop.name: loop
loop.summary: Decrements the count register and jumps if the result is nonzero.
notes: Uses RCX, ECX, or CX depending on address size.

loope.name: loope
loope.summary: Decrements the count register and jumps if the result is nonzero and ZF is set.
notes: Also written as `loopz`.

loopne.name: loopne
loopne.summary: Decrements the count register and jumps if the result is nonzero and ZF is clear.
notes: Also written as `loopnz`.

ltr.name: ltr
ltr.summary: Loads the task register with a task-state segment selector.
notes: Privileged instruction used during OS setup.

lzcnt.name: lzcnt
lzcnt.summary: Counts leading zero bits in the source operand.
notes: Returns operand size when the source is zero.

maxpd.name: maxpd
maxpd.subtitle: Maximum of packed double-precision floating-point values
maxpd.summary: Selects the larger packed double-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

maxps.name: maxps
maxps.subtitle: Maximum of packed single-precision floating-point values
maxps.summary: Selects the larger packed single-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

maxsd.name: maxsd
maxsd.subtitle: Maximum of scalar double-precision floating-point values
maxsd.summary: Selects the larger scalar double-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

maxss.name: maxss
maxss.subtitle: Maximum of scalar single-precision floating-point values
maxss.summary: Selects the larger scalar single-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

mfence.name: mfence
mfence.summary: Serializes memory load and store operations.
notes: Useful when explicit memory ordering is needed.

minpd.name: minpd
minpd.subtitle: Minimum of packed double-precision floating-point values
minpd.summary: Selects the smaller packed double-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

minps.name: minps
minps.subtitle: Minimum of packed single-precision floating-point values
minps.summary: Selects the smaller packed single-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

minsd.name: minsd
minsd.subtitle: Minimum of scalar double-precision floating-point values
minsd.summary: Selects the smaller scalar double-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

minss.name: minss
minss.subtitle: Minimum of scalar single-precision floating-point values
minss.summary: Selects the smaller scalar single-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

mov.name: mov
mov.summary: Copies data from the source operand to the destination operand.
notes: The source operand is not modified.
Most forms do not allow both operands to be memory operands.

movapd.name: movapd
movapd.summary: Moves aligned packed double-precision floating-point data.
notes: Memory operands must be 16-byte aligned for aligned forms.

movaps.name: movaps
movaps.summary: Moves aligned packed single-precision floating-point data.
notes: Memory operands must be 16-byte aligned for aligned forms.

movd.name: movd
movd.summary: Moves a 32-bit integer between a general-purpose register or memory operand and an MMX/XMM register.
notes: Often used to transfer scalar integer data into vector registers.

movdqa.name: movdqa
notes: Memory operands must be suitably aligned for aligned forms.

movdqu.name: movdqu
notes: Use when 16-byte alignment is not guaranteed.

movhlps.name: movhlps
movhlps.summary: Moves two high packed single-precision values from the source to the low half of the destination.
notes: Used for vector rearrangement or specialized memory transfers.

movhpd.name: movhpd
movhpd.summary: Moves a high packed double-precision element to or from memory.
notes: Used for vector rearrangement or specialized memory transfers.

movhps.name: movhps
movhps.summary: Moves high packed single-precision elements to or from memory.
notes: Used for vector rearrangement or specialized memory transfers.

movlhps.name: movlhps
movlhps.summary: Moves two low packed single-precision values from the source into the high half of the destination.
notes: Used for vector rearrangement or specialized memory transfers.

movlpd.name: movlpd
movlpd.summary: Moves a low packed double-precision element to or from memory.
notes: Used for vector rearrangement or specialized memory transfers.

movlps.name: movlps
movlps.summary: Moves low packed single-precision elements to or from memory.
notes: Used for vector rearrangement or specialized memory transfers.

movmskpd.name: movmskpd
movmskpd.summary: Extracts sign bits from packed double-precision lanes into a general-purpose register.
notes: Used for vector rearrangement or specialized memory transfers.

movmskps.name: movmskps
movmskps.summary: Extracts sign bits from packed single-precision lanes into a general-purpose register.
notes: Used for vector rearrangement or specialized memory transfers.

movntdq.name: movntdq
movntdq.summary: Stores packed integer data with a non-temporal memory hint.
notes: Used for vector rearrangement or specialized memory transfers.

movntpd.name: movntpd
movntpd.summary: Stores packed double-precision data with a non-temporal memory hint.
notes: Used for vector rearrangement or specialized memory transfers.

movntps.name: movntps
movntps.summary: Stores packed single-precision data with a non-temporal memory hint.
notes: Used for vector rearrangement or specialized memory transfers.

movntq.name: movntq
movntq.summary: Stores 64-bit MMX data with a non-temporal memory hint.
notes: Used for vector rearrangement or specialized memory transfers.

movq.name: movq
movq.summary: Moves a 64-bit quantity between MMX/XMM registers or memory.
notes: Also used by MMX forms.

movs.name: movs
movs.summary: Copies one element from [RSI] to [RDI] and updates both pointers.
notes: Element size is selected by the operand-size attribute or by using an explicit suffix.

movsb.name: movsb
movsb.summary: Copies one byte from [RSI] to [RDI] and updates both pointers.
notes: Direction depends on DF; `cld` makes pointers advance.

movsd.name: movsd
movsd.summary: Either moves one doubleword string element (`movsd` with no operands) or moves a scalar double-precision floating-point value in SSE forms.
notes: NASM/x86 overloads this mnemonic; operands determine the instruction form.

movshdup.name: movshdup
movshdup.summary: Duplicates odd-index single-precision lanes within a vector.
notes: Used for vector rearrangement or specialized memory transfers.

movsldup.name: movsldup
movsldup.summary: Duplicates even-index single-precision lanes within a vector.
notes: Used for vector rearrangement or specialized memory transfers.

movsq.name: movsq
movsq.summary: Copies one quadword from [RSI] to [RDI] and updates both pointers.
notes: Direction depends on DF; `cld` makes pointers advance.

movss.name: movss
movss.summary: Moves a scalar single-precision floating-point value.
notes: Only the low 32-bit floating-point element is the scalar value.

movsw.name: movsw
movsw.summary: Copies one word from [RSI] to [RDI] and updates both pointers.
notes: Direction depends on DF; `cld` makes pointers advance.

movsx.name: movsx
movsx.summary: Moves a smaller integer operand into a larger register and sign-extends it.
notes: Useful when loading signed bytes or words into wider registers.

movsxd.name: movsxd
movsxd.summary: Moves a 32-bit signed value into a 64-bit register and sign-extends it.
notes: Common when a 32-bit signed integer must become a 64-bit address or integer.

movupd.name: movupd
movupd.summary: Moves unaligned packed double-precision floating-point data.
notes: Use aligned forms only when alignment is guaranteed.

movups.name: movups
movups.summary: Moves unaligned packed single-precision floating-point data.
notes: Use aligned forms only when alignment is guaranteed.

movzx.name: movzx
movzx.summary: Moves a smaller integer operand into a larger register and zero-extends it.
notes: Useful when loading unsigned bytes or words into wider registers.

mul.name: mul
mul.summary: Performs unsigned integer multiplication using the accumulator register.
notes: For 64-bit operands, multiplies `rax` by the source and stores the 128-bit result in `rdx:rax`.
Use `imul` for signed multiplication.

mulpd.name: mulpd
mulpd.subtitle: Multiply packed double-precision floating-point values
mulpd.summary: Multiplies packed double-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

mulps.name: mulps
mulps.subtitle: Multiply packed single-precision floating-point values
mulps.summary: Multiplies packed single-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

mulsd.name: mulsd
mulsd.subtitle: Multiply scalar double-precision floating-point values
mulsd.summary: Multiplies scalar double-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

mulss.name: mulss
mulss.subtitle: Multiply scalar single-precision floating-point values
mulss.summary: Multiplies scalar single-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

mulx.name: mulx
mulx.summary: Performs unsigned multiplication and writes the high and low halves to explicit destinations.
notes: BMI2 instruction; input accumulator is RDX for the implicit multiplicand.

neg.name: neg
neg.summary: Replaces the operand with its two's-complement negation.
notes: Sets CF when the original operand was nonzero.

nop.name: nop
notes: Used for alignment, patch space, or timing padding.

not.name: not
not.summary: Flips all bits in the operand.
notes: Does not update flags.

or.name: or
or.summary: Computes bitwise OR and stores the result in the destination operand.
notes: Often used to set bits.

orpd.name: orpd
orpd.summary: Computes bitwise OR on packed double-precision floating-point vector data.
notes: The operation treats the vector bits directly; it does not perform numeric floating-point arithmetic.

orps.name: orps
orps.summary: Computes bitwise OR on packed single-precision floating-point vector data.
notes: The operation treats the vector bits directly; it does not perform numeric floating-point arithmetic.

out.name: out
out.summary: Writes AL, AX, or EAX to an I/O port.
notes: Privileged or restricted in most protected operating systems.

paddb.name: paddb
notes: Operates lane-wise.

paddd.name: paddd
notes: Operates lane-wise.

paddq.name: paddq
notes: Operates lane-wise.

paddw.name: paddw
notes: Operates lane-wise.

pand.name: pand
pand.summary: Computes packed bitwise AND on MMX/XMM integer vector data.
notes: Operates on vector bits, not numeric lanes.

pandn.name: pandn
pandn.summary: Computes packed bitwise AND NOT on MMX/XMM integer vector data.
notes: Equivalent to `destination = (~destination) & source`.

pause.name: pause
pause.summary: Hints that the current spin-wait loop is intentionally waiting.
notes: Improves behavior of busy-wait loops on many CPUs.

pdep.name: pdep
pdep.summary: Deposits low bits from a source into positions selected by a mask.
notes: BMI2 instruction useful for bit packing.

pext.name: pext
pext.summary: Extracts bits selected by a mask and packs them densely in the result.
notes: BMI2 instruction useful for bit unpacking.

pop.name: pop
pop.summary: Loads a value from the stack and adjusts the stack pointer.
notes: The destination can be a register or memory operand.

popcnt.name: popcnt
popcnt.summary: Counts the number of set bits in the source operand.
notes: Useful for bitsets and masks.

popf.name: popf
popf.summary: Pops a value from the stack into FLAGS/EFLAGS.
notes: Use `popfq` for the 64-bit RFLAGS form in 64-bit code.

popfq.name: popfq
popfq.summary: Pops a value from the stack into the 64-bit RFLAGS register.
notes: Privileged or reserved flag bits may not be changed in user mode.

por.name: por
por.summary: Computes packed bitwise OR on MMX/XMM integer vector data.
notes: Operates on vector bits, not numeric lanes.

psubb.name: psubb
notes: Operates lane-wise.

psubd.name: psubd
notes: Operates lane-wise.

psubq.name: psubq
notes: Operates lane-wise.

psubw.name: psubw
notes: Operates lane-wise.

push.name: push
push.summary: Stores an operand on the stack and adjusts the stack pointer.
notes: In 64-bit mode, stack pushes are normally 8 bytes.

pushf.name: pushf
pushf.summary: Pushes the FLAGS/EFLAGS register onto the stack.
notes: Use `pushfq` for the 64-bit RFLAGS form in 64-bit code.

pushfq.name: pushfq
pushfq.summary: Pushes the 64-bit RFLAGS register onto the stack.
notes: Often paired with `popfq` when saving and restoring flags.

pxor.name: pxor
pxor.summary: Computes packed bitwise XOR on MMX/XMM integer vector data.
notes: Commonly used to zero an XMM register.

rcl.name: rcl
rcl.summary: Rotates bits left through the carry flag.
notes: CF participates as an extra bit in the rotation.

rcr.name: rcr
rcr.summary: Rotates bits right through the carry flag.
notes: CF participates as an extra bit in the rotation.

rdmsr.name: rdmsr
rdmsr.summary: Reads a model-specific register selected by ECX into EDX:EAX.
notes: Privileged instruction used by kernels and firmware.

rdrand.name: rdrand
rdrand.summary: Reads a hardware-generated random value into the destination operand.
notes: CF is set when a random value was returned.

rdseed.name: rdseed
rdseed.summary: Reads a hardware-generated seed value into the destination operand.
notes: CF is set when a seed value was returned.

rdtsc.name: rdtsc
rdtsc.summary: Reads the processor time-stamp counter into EDX:EAX.
notes: Use serialization such as `lfence` or `cpuid` when precise ordering matters.

rdtscp.name: rdtscp
rdtscp.summary: Reads the time-stamp counter and processor ID with partial ordering.
notes: Returns the counter in EDX:EAX and auxiliary data in ECX.

rep.name: rep
rep.summary: Repeats a string instruction while the count register is nonzero.
notes: Uses RCX, ECX, or CX depending on address size.

repe.name: repe
repe.summary: Repeats a string compare/scan while the count register is nonzero and ZF is set.
notes: Also written as `repz`.

repne.name: repne
repne.summary: Repeats a string compare/scan while the count register is nonzero and ZF is clear.
notes: Also written as `repnz`.

ret.name: ret
ret.summary: Returns from a procedure by popping the saved return address.
notes: The optional immediate adjusts the stack after returning.

retf.name: retf
retf.summary: Performs a far return by popping an instruction pointer and code segment.
notes: Mostly used in low-level or legacy segmented code, not normal 64-bit user functions.

rol.name: rol
rol.summary: Rotates bits left without using the carry flag as part of the value.
notes: Bits shifted out of the high end re-enter at the low end.

ror.name: ror
ror.summary: Rotates bits right without using the carry flag as part of the value.
notes: Bits shifted out of the low end re-enter at the high end.

rorx.name: rorx
rorx.summary: Rotates bits right by an immediate count without changing flags.
notes: BMI2 instruction useful in hash and crypto code.

sahf.name: sahf
sahf.summary: Stores AH into selected status flags.
notes: Restores SF, ZF, AF, PF, and CF from AH.

sar.name: sar
sar.summary: Shifts bits right arithmetically, preserving the sign bit.
notes: Useful for signed integer division by powers of two.

sarx.name: sarx
sarx.summary: Performs variable-count arithmetic right shift without changing flags.
notes: BMI2 instruction; count is read from a general-purpose register.

sbb.name: sbb
sbb.summary: Subtracts the source operand and the carry flag from the destination operand.
notes: Used for multi-precision subtraction after `sub` or `cmp`.

scas.name: scas
scas.summary: Compares the accumulator with [RDI], updates flags, and updates RDI.
notes: Element size is selected by the operand-size attribute or by using an explicit suffix.

scasb.name: scasb
scasb.summary: Compares the accumulator with one byte at [RDI], updates flags, and updates RDI.
notes: Often used to scan memory for a value.

scasd.name: scasd
scasd.summary: Compares the accumulator with one doubleword at [RDI], updates flags, and updates RDI.
notes: Often used to scan memory for a value.

scasq.name: scasq
scasq.summary: Compares the accumulator with one quadword at [RDI], updates flags, and updates RDI.
notes: Often used to scan memory for a value.

scasw.name: scasw
scasw.summary: Compares the accumulator with one word at [RDI], updates flags, and updates RDI.
notes: Often used to scan memory for a value.

seta.name: seta
seta.summary: Sets an 8-bit destination to 1 if unsigned above is true, otherwise 0.
notes: The destination is always an 8-bit register or byte memory operand.

setae.name: setae
setae.summary: Sets an 8-bit destination to 1 if unsigned above or equal is true, otherwise 0.
notes: The destination is always an 8-bit register or byte memory operand.

setb.name: setb
setb.summary: Sets an 8-bit destination to 1 if unsigned below is true, otherwise 0.
notes: The destination is always an 8-bit register or byte memory operand.

setbe.name: setbe
setbe.summary: Sets an 8-bit destination to 1 if unsigned below or equal is true, otherwise 0.
notes: The destination is always an 8-bit register or byte memory operand.

setg.name: setg
setg.summary: Sets an 8-bit destination to 1 if signed greater is true, otherwise 0.
notes: The destination is always an 8-bit register or byte memory operand.

setge.name: setge
setge.summary: Sets an 8-bit destination to 1 if signed greater or equal is true, otherwise 0.
notes: The destination is always an 8-bit register or byte memory operand.

setl.name: setl
setl.summary: Sets an 8-bit destination to 1 if signed less is true, otherwise 0.
notes: The destination is always an 8-bit register or byte memory operand.

setle.name: setle
setle.summary: Sets an 8-bit destination to 1 if signed less or equal is true, otherwise 0.
notes: The destination is always an 8-bit register or byte memory operand.

setno.name: setno
setno.summary: Sets an 8-bit destination to 1 if not overflow is true, otherwise 0.
notes: The destination is always an 8-bit register or byte memory operand.

setnp.name: setnp
setnp.summary: Sets an 8-bit destination to 1 if parity odd is true, otherwise 0.
notes: The destination is always an 8-bit register or byte memory operand.

setns.name: setns
setns.summary: Sets an 8-bit destination to 1 if not negative/not sign is true, otherwise 0.
notes: The destination is always an 8-bit register or byte memory operand.

setnz.name: setnz
setnz.summary: Sets an 8-bit destination to 1 if not zero/not equal is true, otherwise 0.
notes: The destination is always an 8-bit register or byte memory operand.

seto.name: seto
seto.summary: Sets an 8-bit destination to 1 if overflow is true, otherwise 0.
notes: The destination is always an 8-bit register or byte memory operand.

setp.name: setp
setp.summary: Sets an 8-bit destination to 1 if parity even is true, otherwise 0.
notes: The destination is always an 8-bit register or byte memory operand.

sets.name: sets
sets.summary: Sets an 8-bit destination to 1 if negative/sign is true, otherwise 0.
notes: The destination is always an 8-bit register or byte memory operand.

setz.name: setz
setz.summary: Sets an 8-bit destination to 1 if zero/equal is true, otherwise 0.
notes: The destination is always an 8-bit register or byte memory operand.

sfence.name: sfence
sfence.summary: Serializes store operations before later stores.
notes: Often used with non-temporal stores.

sgdt.name: sgdt
sgdt.summary: Stores the global descriptor table register to memory.
notes: Low-level system instruction.

sha1msg1.name: sha1msg1
sha1msg1.summary: Performs part of SHA-1 message schedule generation.
notes: Used by optimized cryptographic implementations.

sha1msg2.name: sha1msg2
sha1msg2.summary: Performs part of SHA-1 message schedule generation.
notes: Used by optimized cryptographic implementations.

sha1nexte.name: sha1nexte
sha1nexte.summary: Calculates SHA-1 next E state helper values.
notes: Used by optimized cryptographic implementations.

sha1rnds4.name: sha1rnds4
sha1rnds4.summary: Performs four rounds of SHA-1 using an immediate round selector.
notes: Used by optimized cryptographic implementations.

sha256msg1.name: sha256msg1
sha256msg1.summary: Performs part of SHA-256 message schedule generation.
notes: Used by optimized cryptographic implementations.

sha256msg2.name: sha256msg2
sha256msg2.summary: Performs part of SHA-256 message schedule generation.
notes: Used by optimized cryptographic implementations.

sha256rnds2.name: sha256rnds2
sha256rnds2.summary: Performs two rounds of SHA-256 compression.
notes: Used by optimized cryptographic implementations.

shl.name: shl
shl.summary: Shifts bits left, filling low bits with zero.
notes: Equivalent to `sal` for integer values.

shld.name: shld
shld.summary: Shifts the destination left while pulling bits in from a source operand.
notes: Often used when shifting multi-register integers.

shlx.name: shlx
shlx.summary: Performs variable-count logical left shift without changing flags.
notes: BMI2 instruction; count is read from a general-purpose register.

shr.name: shr
shr.summary: Shifts bits right logically, filling high bits with zero.
notes: Use `sar` for signed arithmetic right shift.

shrd.name: shrd
shrd.summary: Shifts the destination right while pulling bits in from a source operand.
notes: Often used when shifting multi-register integers.

shrx.name: shrx
shrx.summary: Performs variable-count logical right shift without changing flags.
notes: BMI2 instruction; count is read from a general-purpose register.

sidt.name: sidt
sidt.summary: Stores the interrupt descriptor table register to memory.
notes: Low-level system instruction.

sqrtpd.name: sqrtpd
sqrtpd.subtitle: Square root of packed double-precision floating-point values
sqrtpd.summary: Computes square roots of packed double-precision floating-point values from the source operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

sqrtps.name: sqrtps
sqrtps.subtitle: Square root of packed single-precision floating-point values
sqrtps.summary: Computes square roots of packed single-precision floating-point values from the source operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

sqrtsd.name: sqrtsd
sqrtsd.subtitle: Square root of scalar double-precision floating-point values
sqrtsd.summary: Computes square roots of scalar double-precision floating-point values from the source operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

sqrtss.name: sqrtss
sqrtss.subtitle: Square root of scalar single-precision floating-point values
sqrtss.summary: Computes square roots of scalar single-precision floating-point values from the source operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

stc.name: stc
notes: Can seed carry-dependent arithmetic.

std.name: std
std.summary: Sets the direction flag so string instructions decrement addresses.
notes: Remember to restore the expected direction with `cld` before returning to normal code.

sti.name: sti
sti.summary: Sets the interrupt flag, enabling maskable interrupts.
notes: Privileged in normal operating-system environments.

stos.name: stos
stos.summary: Stores the accumulator element into [RDI] and updates RDI.
notes: Element size is selected by the operand-size attribute or by using an explicit suffix.

stosb.name: stosb
stosb.summary: Stores the 8-bit accumulator value into [RDI] and updates RDI.
notes: Direction depends on DF; `cld` makes RDI advance.

stosd.name: stosd
stosd.summary: Stores the 32-bit accumulator value into [RDI] and updates RDI.
notes: Direction depends on DF; `cld` makes RDI advance.

stosq.name: stosq
stosq.summary: Stores the 64-bit accumulator value into [RDI] and updates RDI.
notes: Direction depends on DF; `cld` makes RDI advance.

stosw.name: stosw
stosw.summary: Stores the 16-bit accumulator value into [RDI] and updates RDI.
notes: Direction depends on DF; `cld` makes RDI advance.

str.name: str
str.summary: Stores the task register selector to a register or memory operand.
notes: Low-level system instruction.

sub.name: sub
sub.summary: Subtracts the source operand from the destination operand.
notes: Stores the difference in the destination operand.

subpd.name: subpd
subpd.subtitle: Subtract packed double-precision floating-point values
subpd.summary: Subtracts packed double-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

subps.name: subps
subps.subtitle: Subtract packed single-precision floating-point values
subps.summary: Subtracts packed single-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

subsd.name: subsd
subsd.subtitle: Subtract scalar double-precision floating-point values
subsd.summary: Subtracts scalar double-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

subss.name: subss
subss.subtitle: Subtract scalar single-precision floating-point values
subss.summary: Subtracts scalar single-precision floating-point values from the source operand into the destination operand.
notes: Operates lane-wise for packed forms.
Scalar forms update only the low floating-point element.

swapgs.name: swapgs
swapgs.summary: Swaps the GS base with the kernel GS base MSR.
notes: Used by kernels on system-call and interrupt entry/exit paths.

syscall.name: syscall
syscall.summary: Enters the operating-system system-call handler in 64-bit mode.
notes: On Linux x86-64, RAX is the syscall number and RDI, RSI, RDX, R10, R8, R9 hold arguments 1 through 6.
The return value is placed in RAX; negative values usually indicate errors.

sysenter.name: sysenter
sysenter.summary: Enters a fast system-call handler using legacy SYSENTER/SYSEXIT machinery.
notes: Mostly relevant to operating-system kernels and compatibility paths.

sysexit.name: sysexit
sysexit.summary: Returns from a SYSENTER system-call handler.
notes: Used by kernels, not normal user-space programs.

sysret.name: sysret
sysret.summary: Returns from a fast system call to user mode.
notes: Used by kernels, not normal user-space programs.

test.name: test
test.summary: Computes a bitwise AND only to update CPU flags. The result is not stored.
notes: Common before `jz`/`jnz` to test whether a value is zero or selected bits are set.

tzcnt.name: tzcnt
tzcnt.summary: Counts trailing zero bits in the source operand.
notes: Returns operand size when the source is zero.

ucomisd.name: ucomisd
ucomisd.summary: Compares scalar double-precision floating-point values and updates integer status flags.
notes: Unordered compare handles quiet NaNs without raising invalid-operation for quiet NaNs.

ucomiss.name: ucomiss
ucomiss.summary: Compares scalar single-precision floating-point values and updates integer status flags.
notes: Unordered compare handles quiet NaNs without raising invalid-operation for quiet NaNs.

ud2.name: ud2
notes: Useful for traps, unreachable code, or debugger breakpoints.

unpckhpd.name: unpckhpd
unpckhpd.summary: Unpacks and interleaves high packed floating-point elements from two operands.
notes: Used to rearrange vector lanes.

unpckhps.name: unpckhps
unpckhps.summary: Unpacks and interleaves high packed floating-point elements from two operands.
notes: Used to rearrange vector lanes.

unpcklpd.name: unpcklpd
unpcklpd.summary: Unpacks and interleaves low packed floating-point elements from two operands.
notes: Used to rearrange vector lanes.

unpcklps.name: unpcklps
unpcklps.summary: Unpacks and interleaves low packed floating-point elements from two operands.
notes: Used to rearrange vector lanes.

vaddps.name: vaddps
vaddps.subtitle: Add packed single-precision floats
vaddps.summary: Adds packed 32-bit floating-point values from two vector operands.
notes: Common AVX instruction.
Operates on XMM/YMM/ZMM registers depending on form.

vdivps.name: vdivps
vdivps.subtitle: Divide packed single-precision floats
vdivps.summary: Divides packed 32-bit floating-point values from two vector operands.
notes: Common AVX instruction.
Operates on XMM/YMM/ZMM registers depending on form.

vmovaps.name: vmovaps
vmovaps.summary: Moves aligned packed single-precision floating-point vector data using VEX/EVEX encoding.
notes: Available width depends on CPU feature level.
Aligned memory forms require suitable alignment.

vmovdqa.name: vmovdqa
vmovdqa.summary: Moves aligned packed integer vector data using VEX/EVEX encoding.
notes: Available width depends on CPU feature level.
Aligned memory forms require suitable alignment.

vmovdqu.name: vmovdqu
vmovdqu.summary: Moves unaligned packed integer vector data using VEX/EVEX encoding.
notes: Available width depends on CPU feature level.
Use when alignment is not guaranteed.

vmovups.name: vmovups
vmovups.summary: Moves unaligned packed single-precision floating-point vector data using VEX/EVEX encoding.
notes: Available width depends on CPU feature level.
Use when alignment is not guaranteed.

vmulps.name: vmulps
vmulps.subtitle: Multiplie packed single-precision floats
vmulps.summary: Multiplies packed 32-bit floating-point values from two vector operands.
notes: Common AVX instruction.
Operates on XMM/YMM/ZMM registers depending on form.

vpxor.name: vpxor
vpxor.summary: Computes bitwise XOR on packed integer vector data using AVX encoding.
notes: Common way to zero a vector register.

vsubps.name: vsubps
vsubps.subtitle: Subtract packed single-precision floats
vsubps.summary: Subtracts packed 32-bit floating-point values from two vector operands.
notes: Common AVX instruction.
Operates on XMM/YMM/ZMM registers depending on form.

vxorps.name: vxorps
vxorps.summary: Computes bitwise XOR on packed single-precision vector data using AVX encoding.
notes: Common way to zero a vector register.

vzeroall.name: vzeroall
vzeroall.summary: Zeros all XMM/YMM registers and marks upper vector state clean.
notes: Useful before switching away from AVX-heavy code in some contexts.

vzeroupper.name: vzeroupper
vzeroupper.summary: Zeros the upper halves of YMM registers to avoid AVX/SSE transition penalties.
notes: Common before calling SSE-only code from AVX code.

wait.name: wait
wait.summary: Waits for pending x87 floating-point exceptions.
notes: Also known as `fwait`; mostly relevant to legacy x87 exception handling.

wrmsr.name: wrmsr
wrmsr.summary: Writes EDX:EAX to a model-specific register selected by ECX.
notes: Privileged instruction used by kernels and firmware.

xadd.name: xadd
xadd.summary: Exchanges operands and stores their sum in the destination.
notes: With a memory destination, `lock xadd` is a common atomic fetch-add pattern.

xchg.name: xchg
xchg.summary: Exchanges the values of two operands.
notes: A memory operand form has atomic behavior on x86.

xlat.name: xlat
xlat.summary: Loads a byte from a table addressed by RBX plus AL into AL.
notes: Uses AL as an unsigned index into the table base in RBX/EBX/BX.

xor.name: xor
xor.summary: Computes bitwise exclusive OR and stores the result in the destination operand.
notes: `xor reg, reg` is a common way to zero a register.

xorpd.name: xorpd
xorpd.summary: Computes bitwise XOR on packed double-precision floating-point vector data.
notes: The operation treats the vector bits directly; it does not perform numeric floating-point arithmetic.

xorps.name: xorps
xorps.summary: Computes bitwise XOR on packed single-precision floating-point vector data.
notes: The operation treats the vector bits directly; it does not perform numeric floating-point arithmetic.
